A Maricopa County Flood Control District Stream Gauge at Sonoran Wash showed water levels rising above alarm stage, which is 2.0 feet, at 3:59 AM MST. The unbridged crossing at North Valley Parkway approximately 1 mile downstream from the stream gauge would be affected by the flooding originating from the wash. Based on the travel time chart provided by the MCFCD, it would take approximately 20-30 minutes for the water to reach North Valley Parkway.

*** 1 FATAL *** Man found deceased after pickup truck he was driving was washed away from the floodwaters along Waterman Wash. Pickup truck was found less than a mile downstream with the driver not found, requiring a search and rescue operation to be activated. Man was found by the search and rescue team approximately 34 hours after the reported incident.

***DELAYED REPORT*** Public sent a video of a brief tornado near the intersection of HWY 60 and N 439th Avenue in Forepaugh, AZ. This tornado crossed HWY 60 and produced EF-0 damage at a property on the north side of the highway. Damage included a flipped RV trailer, minor damage to other vehicles, and loss of a garage roof. Wind speeds with this tornado were estimated between 80-85 mph. Time estimated by radar.
***Delayed Report*** Eagle Eye Road closed due to flooding likely due to several inundated low water crossings. A stream gauge along Tiger Wash exceeded alarm stage and was used for timing.
